summary: A high‑quality soundboard capture of the legendary 12/06/73 show, highlighted by an epic Here Comes Sunshine and a towering Dark Star, with generally good audio despite minor missing intros.
review: The performance is widely praised – set two shines with a marathon Here Comes Sunshine, a soaring Dark Star, Big River and Eyes – while the first set delivers solid classics. The Miller soundboard upgrade sounds clear and balanced, though listeners note a missing ~20‑second intro to Dark Star and some late‑show mix issues with Bob’s guitar. Overall the recording offers an excellent historical snapshot of one of ’73’s most talked‑about concerts.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 5‑star, highlighting superb musical moments and a clear soundboard source; only isolated technical quirks (missing intro, fade‑out on guitar) keep the rating just below perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced mix with a recent patch fixing the end of Here Comes Sunshine. Missing first ~20 seconds of Dark Star and the very start of Bertha are noted, as is a later reduction in Bob Weir's guitar presence.

poor_songs: Beat It On Down The Line
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rolls through classic 1973 material with a few flat spots, but the second set erupts into an unforgettable journey – Ramble on Rose and Me & My Uncle open the set, leading to the marathon Here Comes Sunshine, a powerful Dark Star > Eyes sequence, and a fiery Big River.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring guitar tone on Here Comes Sunshine and Dark Star; his vocals are noted as especially clear on Ramble on Rose.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass is described as echoing beautifully (especially after China>Rider) and having a “fuzz‑tone” quality that recalls early‑70s jazz fusion sounds.
band_member_comments.Bob: Early in the show he adds strong rhythm and commentary; later his guitar fades in the mix, which some listeners find disappointing.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is highlighted for sounding nice on piano during Big River and switching to Rhodes mid‑Dark Star, adding texture to the jam.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy (Bill Kreutzmann) “beats his drum kit to death” on Big River and provides a driving pulse throughout the second set.
